narrativeOwner: Prof. Thamar Solorio, Vice Provost of Faculty Excellence and Advancement

assessment.headline: The university is deliberately slowing its rapid faculty growth to focus on quality improvement. Faculty hiring is capped at 140 — with 128 currently signed, at most 12 new hires are possible this year. Hiring standards are being recalibrated through a five-score evaluation targeting Top 30 US universities, aiming to sustain the university's prestige and credibility as it matures.
whereWeStand: The March 9 SMT imposed a hard cap at 140 faculty — only 12 slots remain.
A new five-score evaluation framework targeting Top 30 US universities is being developed. Several pipeline candidates are being reassessed, and some will be declined under the higher bar.
Highest-profile pipeline candidate is a senior researcher from DeepMind (h-index 52) — significantly above current faculty average. A competing counter-offer is expected.

whatsNext.text: The five-score framework must be finalized and communicated to deans before hiring resumes.
New faculty offer letters with revised startup packages are expected this month. Faculty currently hold letters with outdated terms.
The tenure and promotion framework (Top 20-calibrated) is complete and awaiting presidential review.
RAIS program (Emirati faculty pipeline) advertising is live. First candidates are not expected until late 2026, but government stakeholders may ask about progress.
whatsNext.owner: VP Faculty Excellence & VP Government Relations

facultyAchievements.title: Elected to the French Academy of Sciences
facultyAchievements.description: Elected as a member of the French Academy of Sciences for contributions to statistical learning theory and stochastic optimization.
facultyAchievements.significance: First MBZUAI faculty member elected to a national academy. Strong signal for institutional prestige.

facultyAchievements.title: ACL 2026 Outstanding Reviewer Award
facultyAchievements.description: Recognized as an outstanding reviewer for the ACL 2026 main conference for exceptional quality and thoroughness of reviews.
facultyAchievements.significance: Recognized for service to a flagship language-AI venue. Builds MBZUAI's visibility in the global language technology community.

facultyAchievements.title: Invited keynote at CVPR 2026
facultyAchievements.description: Invited keynote speaker on 'The Future of Visual Foundation Models' at CVPR 2026, the premier computer vision conference.
facultyAchievements.significance: CVPR keynote is a top-tier visibility opportunity. Positions MBZUAI faculty among the leading voices in computer vision.

facultyAchievements.title: Appointed to WHO Expert Advisory Panel on AI in Healthcare
facultyAchievements.description: Appointed to the WHO Expert Advisory Panel on Artificial Intelligence in Healthcare, advising on governance and deployment of AI in global health systems.
facultyAchievements.significance: International governance role. Connects MBZUAI to global health AI policy conversations.

facultyAchievements.title: Elevated to IEEE Fellow
facultyAchievements.description: Elevated to IEEE Fellow for contributions to efficient computing architectures for deep learning systems.
facultyAchievements.significance: IEEE Fellowship is one of the most prestigious recognitions in electrical engineering and computer science.

facultyProfiles.name: Dr. Sarah Chen
topPublications: Diffusion Models for Molecular Generation — Nature Machine Intelligence, 2025
Scalable Variational Inference for Large-Scale Bayesian Networks — NeurIPS 2024 (Best Paper)
Foundation Models for Drug Discovery — ICML 2024
facultyProfiles.bio: Dr. Chen leads the Probabilistic AI Lab at Stanford, focusing on generative models for scientific applications. Her work on diffusion models for molecular generation has been widely cited and adopted by pharmaceutical companies.
facultyProfiles.hiringNotes: Strong candidate aligned with the Biological and Life Sciences cross-disciplinary push. Compensation negotiation in progress — competing offer from Google DeepMind.

facultyProfiles.name: Dr. Ahmed Al-Rashidi
topPublications: Communication-Efficient Federated Learning at Scale — OSDI 2025
Differential Privacy in Production ML Systems — IEEE S&P 2024
Secure Multi-Party Computation for Healthcare — USENIX Security 2024
facultyProfiles.bio: Dr. Al-Rashidi is an Assistant Professor at ETH Zurich specializing in privacy-preserving distributed systems. His work on federated learning has been adopted by multiple European healthcare institutions.
facultyProfiles.hiringNotes: Offer extended Feb 28. Strong systems background fills a gap in Computing and Mathematical Sciences. Response expected by March 20.

facultyProfiles.name: Dr. Maria Petrova
topPublications: Massively Multilingual Foundation Models — ACL 2025
Zero-Shot Cross-Lingual Understanding — EMNLP 2024
Efficient Adaptation for Low-Resource Languages — NAACL 2024
awards: Canada Research Chair in Multilingual AI (2023)
facultyProfiles.bio: Dr. Petrova is a leading researcher in multilingual language AI with a focus on low-resource languages, particularly Arabic and Slavic languages. She joins MBZUAI from the University of Toronto where she led the Multilingual AI Lab.
facultyProfiles.hiringNotes: Signed January 2026. Start date: August 2026. Key hire for Arabic language modeling — directly relevant to JAIS model development.

facultyProfiles.name: Dr. James Okonkwo
topPublications: Cooperative Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning in Complex Environments — Nature 2025
Scalable Alignment Methods for Large Language Models — ICML 2025
Reward Modeling Under Distribution Shift — NeurIPS 2024
facultyProfiles.bio: Dr. Okonkwo has spent 7 years at DeepMind working on reinforcement learning and AI safety. His recent work on cooperative multi-agent systems was published in Nature.
facultyProfiles.hiringNotes: Highest-profile candidate in current pipeline. h-index of 52 exceeds current faculty average by significant margin. Compensation package is complex — DeepMind counter-offer expected.

facultyProfiles.name: Dr. Yuki Tanaka
topPublications: Real-Time Neural Scene Reconstruction for Autonomous Systems — CVPR 2025
Efficient 3D Gaussian Splatting — ECCV 2024
Visual Foundation Models for Robotics — ICRA 2024
facultyProfiles.bio: Dr. Tanaka specializes in 3D computer vision with applications in autonomous systems and robotics. His lab at the University of Tokyo has produced several widely-used open-source tools for neural scene reconstruction.
facultyProfiles.hiringNotes: Signed February 2026. Start date: September 2026. Strengthens Engineering and Applied Sciences robotics capability — relevant to Agentic Robotics Lab.

facultyProfiles.name: Dr. Fatima Al-Hashemi
topPublications: Deep Learning for Rare Disease Variant Interpretation — Nature Genetics 2025
Protein-Ligand Binding Prediction with Graph Neural Networks — Bioinformatics 2024
Multi-Omics Integration for Precision Medicine — Cell Systems 2024
awards: L'Oréal-UNESCO For Women in Science Fellowship (2024)
facultyProfiles.bio: Dr. Al-Hashemi is one of the leading computational genomics researchers in the Gulf region. Her work on AI-driven rare disease diagnosis has direct clinical applications. Emirati national.
facultyProfiles.hiringNotes: Offer extended March 5. Emirati national — important for RAIS program and government reporting. Biological and Life Sciences needs to grow. Waiting on response.

facultyProfiles.name: Dr. Raj Patel
topPublications: Compiler-Aware Neural Architecture Search — ASPLOS 2025
Efficient Transformer Inference on Heterogeneous Hardware — MLSys 2024
Automated Kernel Fusion for Deep Learning — OSDI 2024
awards: ACM SIGARCH Dissertation Award Honorable Mention (2021)
facultyProfiles.bio: Dr. Patel's research bridges programming languages and machine learning, focusing on making ML systems faster and more efficient. His compiler optimization work is used in production at several major cloud providers.
facultyProfiles.hiringNotes: Systems expertise is critical for MBZUAI's compute infrastructure goals. Berkeley is countering with a retention package — decision expected mid-March.
